title: Deductions for specified investments and payments
description: Section 80C provides deduction for eligible investments and payments under the old tax regime, subject to an overall cap.
details: Maximum deduction limit is Rs 1,50,000 in aggregate.
Eligible instruments include PPF, ELSS, NSC, life insurance premiums, Sukanya Samriddhi, 5-year tax saver fixed deposits, and repayment of home loan principal.
The deduction is generally claimed under the old tax regime subject to other applicable conditions.

title: Deduction for health insurance premium
description: Section 80D allows deduction for medical insurance premium and preventive health check-ups, subject to age-based limits.
details: Up to Rs 25,000 can be claimed for self, spouse, and dependent children.
An additional deduction up to Rs 25,000 is available for parents, increasing to Rs 50,000 if the parents are senior citizens.
For a taxpayer who is a senior citizen, the self and family limit can increase to Rs 50,000.
Preventive health check-up is allowed within the overall limits, subject to the applicable cap.

title: Deduction for interest on education loan
description: Section 80E allows deduction for interest paid on an education loan for higher education.
details: Deduction is available only for the interest component of the education loan.
There is no monetary ceiling specified in the deduction.
The deduction is available for up to 8 assessment years beginning from the year in which interest repayment starts or until interest is fully paid, whichever is earlier.

title: Deduction for eligible charitable donations
description: Section 80G provides deduction for donations to specified funds and institutions subject to conditions.
details: Depending on the institution, either 50% or 100% of the donation may qualify for deduction.
Some donations are subject to a qualifying limit while others are allowed without limit.
Cash donations in excess of Rs 2,000 are not eligible for deduction under this section.

title: Exemption for house rent allowance
description: Section 10(13A) read with applicable rules provides exemption for HRA subject to prescribed computation.
details: The exemption is generally the least of actual HRA received, rent paid minus 10% of salary, and 50% of salary for metro cities or 40% for non-metro cities.
Salary for this purpose generally includes basic salary and relevant dearness allowance, where applicable.
HRA exemption is relevant where the employee pays rent for residential accommodation.

title: Deduction for interest on borrowed capital for house property
description: Section 24(b) allows deduction for interest on housing loan subject to property-use conditions.
details: For a self-occupied property, interest deduction can be claimed up to Rs 2,00,000 subject to applicable conditions.
For a let-out property, the interest treatment differs and loss set-off limits may separately apply.
Pre-construction interest may be claimed in installments as permitted under the provision.

title: Rebate for resident individuals
description: Section 87A provides a tax rebate to eligible resident individuals below the applicable income threshold.
details: The rebate amount and threshold depend on the regime and the relevant law in force for the year.
Eligibility applies only to resident individuals satisfying the prescribed taxable income threshold.
The exact rebate should be interpreted with reference to the applicable regime and tax year.

title: TDS on interest other than interest on securities
description: Section 194A covers deduction of tax at source on specified interest payments.
details: TDS is generally deducted at 10% on eligible interest payments, subject to PAN and other conditions.
Threshold rules differ based on the payer category and applicable law.
Certain exclusions and declarations may affect whether TDS is actually deducted.

title: Presumptive taxation for eligible businesses
description: Section 44AD offers a presumptive taxation scheme for eligible resident businesses subject to turnover conditions.
details: Presumptive income is generally deemed at 8% of turnover or 6% for eligible digital receipts, subject to conditions.
The scheme applies to eligible assessees carrying on eligible businesses within the prescribed turnover limits.
Maintaining lower declared income may trigger regular books and audit requirements, subject to law.

title: Presumptive taxation for specified professionals
description: Section 44ADA provides a presumptive income scheme for eligible professionals.
details: Income is generally deemed at 50% of gross receipts under the scheme, subject to conditions.
The section applies to specified professions and prescribed gross receipt limits.
Claiming lower income may require maintaining books and complying with audit-related provisions where applicable.

title: Short-term capital gains on certain equity transactions
description: Section 111A prescribes a special tax rate for qualifying short-term capital gains on equity-related transactions.
details: The section applies to qualifying short-term capital gains arising from transfer of certain equity shares, units, or business trust units where conditions such as STT are met.
The special tax rate applicable under this section is 15%, subject to the law in force and surcharge or cess implications.
Whether a transaction qualifies depends on the nature of the asset and the transaction conditions.

title: Long-term capital gains on certain equity transactions
description: Section 112A covers long-term capital gains on specified equity instruments above the exempt threshold.
details: Long-term capital gains exceeding Rs 1,00,000 on qualifying equity shares, equity-oriented mutual funds, and specified units may be taxed at 10%, subject to conditions.
Grandfathering principles may apply to certain older investments as per the statutory framework.
Eligibility depends on the asset type and fulfillment of transaction conditions such as STT where relevant.

title: Exemption for sum received under life insurance policy
description: Section 10(10D) exempts eligible life insurance maturity proceeds subject to policy conditions.
details: Amounts received under a life insurance policy may be exempt, subject to statutory exceptions and conditions.
Policies breaching prescribed premium-to-sum-assured conditions may not qualify for full exemption.
Special rules may apply to certain ULIPs and high-premium policies.

title: New tax regime for individuals and specified taxpayers
description: Section 115BAC governs the concessional new tax regime and its conditions.
details: The new tax regime applies subject to the slab structure and conditions in force for the relevant tax year.
Several deductions and exemptions available in the old regime may not be available or may be restricted under this regime.
The option mechanism and continuity rules can vary depending on taxpayer category.

title: Interest for default or deferment of advance tax
description: Sections 234B and 234C impose interest for non-payment or deferment of advance tax installments.
details: Interest is generally computed at 1% per month or part of a month as applicable under each section.
Section 234B broadly relates to default in payment of advance tax, while Section 234C addresses deferment of installments.
The amount depends on the shortfall and the due-date schedule for advance tax.
